requirements.statement: A requirement id present in the base ref but absent in the change, without an approved supersession, is a hard error (`REQUIREMENT_REMOVED`) — diff-based, deterministic.
requirements.why: Requirements are durable — silently dropping one without an approved supersession must be a deterministic hard error.

requirements.statement: A status flip to `superseded`/`retired` requires the domain owner's approval (CODEOWNERS on the spec path); otherwise gate red.
requirements.why: Retiring or superseding a requirement is a governance act — it needs the owner's approval, enforced via CODEOWNERS.

requirements.statement: Both governance checks run in the standard gate — deterministic, fully-trusted red.
requirements.why: Governance must be part of the ordinary gate, not an optional extra, so the red is fully trusted.
